steps.title: Identify the central paradox
steps.content:
The poem's key signal is the word But at line 5, which creates a stark contrast: despite promising newness—new roads, fresh seeing, renewed lives—the poem then asserts there'll be the same backs bending, / The same sad feet shall drum.

This structure reveals that repetition persists even as circumstances change.

steps.title: Match the paradox to the choices
steps.content:
The poem examines how repetitiveness in human life can be both rewarding—in the fresh perspective and renewal each cycle offers—and challenging, as sadness and struggle endure.

The other choices misread the focus:
  • B targets day/night imagery
  • C invents an emotion-frequency claim
  • D creates a distinction the text doesn't make
.

This matches choice A — A is correct.
